title: Contributing to idem
text: document describes contribution process idem. package follows tidyverse conventions code style workflow — see tidyverse development contributing guide code review principles broader context.

title: Contributing to idem
text: File issue writing code. ensures change needed avoids wasted effort. found bug, include minimal reprex issue — makes easier diagnose write unit test later. tidyverse guide create great issue useful advice.

title: Contributing to idem
text: following tools must available PATH following contribution workflow. Install work across projects.

title: Contributing to idem
text: uv Python package manager used install tools: See uv installation guide Windows options.
code: # macOS / Linux curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

title: Contributing to idem
text: pre-commit runs hook scripts commit. Install via uv (recommended): Alternatives: pip install pre-commit brew install pre-commit.
code: uv tool install pre-commit

title: Contributing to idem
text: repository uses release-branch workflow: release/vX.Y.Z branch created maintainer planned release. piece work (feature, fix, docs, etc.) gets branch cut target release branch, named <issue-number>-brief-description (e.g. 7-improve-contributing-guide). Completed feature branches merged release branch via pull request. CI checks must pass merging. release ready, release branch merged main. merge commit tagged version number (e.g. v0.2.0). Maintainers create manage release branches. external contributor, check whether active release branch exists target PR . none exists, ask issue branch use.
code: gitGraph    commit id: "…"    branch release/v0.2.0    checkout release/v0.2.0     branch "5-add-validation"    checkout "5-add-validation"    commit id: "feat: add validation"    commit id: "test: cover validation"    checkout release/v0.2.0    merge "5-add-validation" id: "Merge #5"     branch "6-fix-edge-case"    checkout "6-fix-edge-case"    commit id: "fix: edge case"    checkout release/v0.2.0    merge "6-fix-edge-case" id: "Merge #6"     checkout main    merge release/v0.2.0 id: "v0.2.0" tag: "v0.2.0"

title: Contributing to idem
text: Fork repository clone locally. easiest way : plain git:
code: usethis::create_from_github("impact-initiatives/idem", fork = TRUE) git clone https://github.com/<your-username>/idem.git cd idem git remote add upstream https://github.com/impact-initiatives/idem.git

what: 2. Install dependencies and verify the baseline
title: Contributing to idem
text: R CMD check must pass cleanly make changes. , open issue rather continuing. moving , install pre-commit hooks — see Pre-commit hooks . hooks must place make first commit.
code: devtools::install_dev_deps() devtools::check()

title: Contributing to idem
text: Create branch active release branch:
code: git fetch upstream git checkout -b <issue-number>-brief-description-of-change upstream/release/vX.Y.Z

title: Contributing to idem
text: Follow commit message format described . commit coherent, self-contained unit work. PR whole address just one thing — see tidyverse guide focused PRs.

title: Contributing to idem
text: ready, push open PR: plain git: open pull request GitHub. PR title must briefly describe change. PR body must contain Fixes #<issue-number>.
code: usethis::pr_push() git push -u origin 7-brief-description-of-change

title: Contributing to idem
text: Pre-commit hooks tests run time attempt commit. tests pass, commit made, otherwise . repository uses pre-commit run automated checks commit. hook scripts committed repository — every contributor must install locally cloning. steps required, optional: without hooks run.

what: 1. Install the precommit R package and activate the hooks
title: Contributing to idem
text: precommit R package provides convenient way install pre-commit framework activate hooks R. Run following R session root cloned repository: precommit::use_precommit() writes hook scripts .git/hooks/. pre-commit framework must already installed — see Prerequisites done yet. need run per clone. [!CAUTION] abort hooks running RStudio git tab. Non-staged changes stashed temp directory abort RStudio, changes brought back repo. — precommit documentation installs pre-commit commit-msg hooks. commit-msg hook enforces Conventional Commits format — see Commit message format rules allowed types.
code: install.packages("precommit") precommit::use_precommit()

title: Contributing to idem
text: run hooks every file without making commit: run single hook id:
code: pre-commit run --all-files pre-commit run <hook-id> --all-files # e.g. pre-commit run lintr --all-files

title: Contributing to idem
text: conventional-pre-commit hook enforces Conventional Commits specification. Every commit message must start type prefix: Types used repository: Example: feat: add validate_choices() function Commits follow format rejected hook.
code: type: short description in the imperative mood

title: Contributing to idem
text: New code must follow tidyverse style guide. air-format hook applies automatically commit — restyle code unrelated change. format manually committing: format single file: lint R source files: package uses roxygen2 Markdown syntax documentation. editing roxygen2 comments, regenerate docs: package uses testthat (v3) unit tests. Contributions include test cases easier accept.
code: air format . air format R/my_file.R lintr::lint_package() devtools::document()

title: XLSForm Comparison and Validation
text: following examples make subset rule concrete demonstrating passing direction check: dev carrying extra content target doesn’t require. every case result empty tibble. four return empty tibbles — issues — confirming dev superset target always allowed.
code: # dev has an extra question that target doesn't need — passes dev_extra_q <- target$survey[1L, ] dev_extra_q$name <- "dev_only_question" dev_with_extra_q <- xlsform(   survey  = rbind(target$survey, dev_extra_q),   choices = target$choices ) validate_question_names(target, dev_with_extra_q) # 0 issues — target is a valid subset #> # A tibble: 0 × 5 #> # ℹ 5 variables: check <chr>, severity <chr>, name <chr>, list_name <chr>, #> #   detail <chr>  # dev defines an extra choice list that target doesn't use — passes dev_extra_list <- target$choices[target$choices$list_name == "l_yn" &   !is.na(target$choices$list_name), ] dev_extra_list$list_name <- "l_dev_only" dev_with_extra_list <- xlsform(   survey  = target$survey,   choices = rbind(target$choices, dev_extra_list) ) validate_list_names(target, dev_with_extra_list) # 0 issues — target need not use every list in dev #> # A tibble: 0 × 5 #> # ℹ 5 variables: check <chr>, severity <chr>, name <chr>, list_name <chr>, #> #   detail <chr>  # dev references an extra list in its survey that target doesn't — passes dev_survey_extra <- target$survey dev_survey_extra$type[dev_survey_extra$type == "text"][1L] <- "select_one l_dev_only" dev_with_extra_ref <- xlsform(   survey  = dev_survey_extra,   choices = rbind(target$choices, dev_extra_list) ) validate_survey_list_names(target, dev_with_extra_ref) # 0 issues — target simply doesn't use that list #> # A tibble: 0 × 5 #> # ℹ 5 variables: check <chr>, severity <chr>, name <chr>, list_name <chr>, #> #   detail <chr>  # dev has an extra choice option in a shared list that target doesn't — passes dev_extra_opt <- target$choices[target$choices$list_name == "l_yn" &   !is.na(target$choices$list_name), ][1L, ] dev_extra_opt$name <- "not_applicable" dev_with_extra_opt <- xlsform(   survey  = target$survey,   choices = rbind(target$choices, dev_extra_opt) ) validate_choices(target, dev_with_extra_opt) # 0 issues — target uses a subset of available options #> # A tibble: 0 × 5 #> # ℹ 5 variables: check <chr>, severity <chr>, name <chr>, list_name <chr>, #> #   detail <chr>

what: Building a target form with issues
title: XLSForm Comparison and Validation
text: practice load forms disk: tutorial construct target content dev missing, introducing one example issue type.
code: target <- read_xlsform("path/to/target.xlsx") dev    <- read_xlsform("path/to/dev.xlsx") # 1. target requires a question dev doesn't have extra_q <- target$survey[1L, ] extra_q$name <- "required_indicator" target_survey <- rbind(target$survey, extra_q)  # 2. target requires a choice option dev is missing (in the 'l_yn' list) extra_opt <- target$choices[target$choices$list_name == "l_yn" &   !is.na(target$choices$list_name), ][1L, ] extra_opt$name <- "mandatory_option"  # 3. target defines a choice list that dev's choices sheet doesn't have new_list <- target$choices[target$choices$list_name == "l_yn" &   !is.na(target$choices$list_name), ][1:2, ] new_list$list_name <- "l_required_scale" new_list$name <- c("low", "high")  target_choices <- rbind(target$choices, extra_opt, new_list)  # 4. target references a list in its survey that dev's survey doesn't have target_survey$type[target_survey$type == "text"][1L] <- "select_one l_required_scale"  # Build a dev form that is the original (without the additions above) # so that it is missing everything target now requires dev <- xlsform(survey = target$survey, choices = target$choices)  # Re-assign target with all the additions target_with_issues <- xlsform(survey = target_survey, choices = target_choices)

title: Get choice options from an XLSForm — xlsform_choices
code: xlsform_choices(x, ...)  # Default S3 method xlsform_choices(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'xlsform' xlsform_choices(x, ...)

title: Get choice options from an XLSForm — xlsform_choices
text: x xlsform object. ... Ignored; present S3 method compatibility.

title: Get choice options from an XLSForm — xlsform_choices
text: named list character vectors. name list name; element character vector option name values list. Rows NA either list_name name silently dropped.

title: Get choice options from an XLSForm — xlsform_choices
text: useful inspecting options available given select_one select_multiple question, used internally validate_choices() compare option sets across two forms.
